claims.proposition: For the declared 2048-value payload, RQ8/32/64 uses fewer element-payload bytes than ordinary Q8 with one binary32 scale per 32-value block.
claims.conclusion: Ordinary Q8 uses 2304 bytes and RQ8 uses 2113 bytes, so RQ8 saves exactly 191 bytes, or 191/2304 (8.289930555555555 percent).
premises: The fixture contains 2048 values in 64 blocks of 32 values.
Ordinary Q8 uses one signed quant byte per value and one four-byte binary32 scale per block.
RQ8/32/64 uses one signed quant byte per value, one numerator byte per block, and one exponent byte for the 64-block group.
claims.countercheck: Changing the ordinary-Q8 scale representation or block layout changes the baseline and requires a new comparison.
claims.uncertainty: None for the integer payload count when the representation premises hold.
claims.boundary: Element payload only; excludes object headers, alignment, temporary storage, executable code, accuracy effects, encoding work, and execution characteristics.

claims.proposition: The declared RQ8/32/64 element payload uses fewer bytes than raw binary32 values for the same 2048-value fixture.
claims.conclusion: Binary32 uses 8192 bytes and RQ8 uses 2113 bytes, so RQ8 saves exactly 6079 bytes, or 6079/8192 (74.20654296875 percent).
premises: A binary32 value occupies four payload bytes.
The RQ8/32/64 component counts total 2113 bytes.
claims.countercheck: The fair quantized baseline is the separate ordinary-Q8 comparison, not this raw-value context row.
claims.uncertainty: None for the integer payload count when the representation premises hold.
claims.boundary: Element payload only; does not define a serialized artifact or establish any system-level conclusion.

claims.proposition: The generated mirror kernel preserves the retained reference outcomes inside the checked 2048-column domain.
claims.conclusion: The deterministic release fixtures produced bit-equal binary32 output in two fresh correctness profiles.
premises: The direct and ASDF mirror suites each passed 37 checks.
Twelve deterministic differential cases passed in two fresh correctness profiles.
The strict candidate disabled the public fallback during differential verification.
claims.warrant: Deterministic differential testing against the retained semantic oracle.
claims.countercheck: Unsupported graph shapes and incomplete targets were separately required to fail closed.
claims.uncertainty: The finite fixtures do not enumerate every valid input bit pattern or SBCL build.
claims.boundary: Does not establish a general compiler, cross-runtime portability, model quality, or stable serialization.

claims.proposition: The alpha.1 release is faster as a whole system.
claims.conclusion: Not established by the available evidence.
premises: The release contains bounded Common Lisp kernels and one symbolic graph slice.
No representative end-to-end system workload was run for this release.
claims.warrant: A whole-system speed claim requires a comparable implementation and representative measured workload.
claims.countercheck: Storage arithmetic and bounded semantic checks cannot establish execution speed.
claims.uncertainty: The missing end-to-end benchmark prevents a supported direction of comparison.
claims.boundary: Future evidence may change this classification; alpha.1 does not.
